title: E2E Smoke Test Documentation
summary: Documentation (docs/e2e.md) for a reproducible manual smoke test against the golemic_e2e sandbox repo. Covers sandbox preparation (.golemic/ setup, throwaway issue creation), test execution (golemic preflight, golemic run --issue N), expected outcome (PR + formal review by two bot identities), and teardown commands.

stakeholder_intent.goal: Run a manual end-to-end smoke test of the golemic loop against a sandbox repo
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Verifies that the full loop (preflight → run → dev → PR → review) works against real GitHub, giving confidence before relying on the tool for production work
stakeholder_intent.trigger: Maintainer has completed Iteration 1 implementation and wants to verify the system works end-to-end
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: A human can follow the smoke test document and successfully execute a full loop, observing a PR with a formal review from two different bot identities

in_scope: Sandbox repo preparation: create golemic_e2e repo, populate .golemic/ config and guidelines, create a throwaway issue
Test execution steps: golemic preflight, golemic run --issue N
Expected outcome: a PR is created and a formal review is submitted by the two bot identities (dev bot creates PR, reviewer bot reviews)
Teardown commands: close PR, delete branch, clean up worktrees
out_of_scope: Automation of the E2E smoke test (CI integration)
Setup of bot tokens or GitHub repos (precondition)

preconditions: golemic binary is built and available
Two bot tokens are configured (dev and reviewer)
A sandbox GitHub repo (golemic_e2e) exists and is cloned locally
Bot tokens have write access to the sandbox repo

business_rules.rule: The smoke test must be documented as a step-by-step procedure that a human can follow without additional knowledge
business_rules.outcome: Every step is explicit: shell commands, expected output, and verification checks

business_rules.rule: The smoke test must include teardown instructions that restore the sandbox repo to a clean state
business_rules.outcome: Teardown section includes: close PR, delete branch, remove worktrees, delete issue

business_rules.rule: The documented expected outcome must include a formal GitHub review (APPROVE or REQUEST_CHANGES) by the reviewer bot, confirming two different identities were involved
business_rules.outcome: The test verifies that the PR author (dev bot) and reviewer (reviewer bot) are different GitHub accounts

business_rules.rule: The smoke test document must instruct the user to create a throwaway issue in the sandbox repo
business_rules.outcome: An issue is created specifically for the smoke test, to be deleted during teardown

interfaces.name: docs/e2e.md
interfaces.operation: Human reads the documentation and executes the smoke test procedure

inputs.name: sandbox repo path
constraints: Must be a git repository with golemic preflight passing

inputs.name: issue number
constraints: Must be a valid issue in the sandbox repo

outputs.body: PR created and formal review submitted by two different bot identities

errors.message: See preflight output for missing prerequisites

process_steps.name: Prepare sandbox repo
process_steps.trigger: Human decides to run the smoke test
process_steps.action: Clone or create golemic_e2e repo, populate .golemic/ directory with valid config.json and guidelines, create a throwaway issue
process_steps.state_before: Sandbox repo does not exist or is empty
process_steps.state_after: Sandbox repo has .golemic/ with valid config and guidelines, and a throwaway issue exists
process_steps.failure_behavior: Document common failure modes (repo not found, missing permissions, etc.)

process_steps.name: Run golemic preflight
process_steps.action: Run golemic preflight from the sandbox repo root, verify all checks pass with SUCCESS
process_steps.state_after: Preflight confirms all prerequisites are met
process_steps.failure_behavior: Document preflight check failures and how to resolve them

process_steps.name: Run golemic run --issue N
process_steps.action: Run golemic run --issue <N> with the throwaway issue number, observe output
process_steps.state_after: Run completes with either success or failure
process_steps.failure_behavior: Document how to interpret run failure output and find logs in runs/<runId>/

process_steps.name: Verify expected outcome
process_steps.action: Check that a PR exists on the sandbox repo, check that the PR has a formal review from the reviewer bot (not the dev bot), verify two different identities involved
process_steps.state_before: Run has completed, outcome is not yet verified
process_steps.state_after: Expected outcome verified or failure identified
process_steps.failure_behavior: Document how to diagnose failures using runs/<runId>/ events.jsonl and transcripts

process_steps.name: Teardown
process_steps.action: Close the PR, delete the branch, remove worktrees, delete the throwaway issue
process_steps.state_after: Sandbox repo is clean (no PR, no branch, no worktrees, no issue)
process_steps.failure_behavior: Document manual cleanup steps if automatic teardown fails

security.authentication: N/A (documentation only; human uses their own credentials and bot tokens)
authorization_rules: Human must have write access to the sandbox repo
Bot tokens must have write access to the sandbox repo

integration_points: gh CLI (for issue creation and verification)
architecture_constraints: The smoke test document must reference the actual golemic binary path and sandbox repo path
All commands must be copy-pasteable from the document
allowed_changes: Create docs/e2e.md with full smoke test procedure
forbidden_changes: Do not add automated test scripts that run as part of go test
Do not require the e2e smoke test to pass for CI
implementation_context.migration_strategy: New documentation file; no migration needed

acceptance_scenarios.title: Document is complete and actionable
given: A human has access to a sandbox repo and configured bot tokens
when: The human follows the smoke test document step by step
then: Every step has a concrete shell command
Expected output is described for each step
The human can complete the smoke test without additional knowledge

acceptance_scenarios.title: Teardown instructions are included
given: The smoke test document is read
when: The human reaches the teardown section
then: Teardown commands are provided for closing the PR, deleting the branch, removing worktrees, and deleting the issue

acceptance_scenarios.title: Expected outcome includes two different bot identities
given: The smoke test document is read
when: The human reads the verification section
then: The document instructs to verify that the PR author and reviewer are different GitHub accounts

acceptance_scenarios.title: Throwaway issue creation is documented
given: The smoke test document is read
when: The human reads the preparation section
then: The document instructs the human to create a throwaway issue
The issue is meant to be deleted during teardown

test_cases: Each shell command is syntactically valid
Teardown section covers all created artifacts
quality_commands: Review docs/e2e.md for completeness and correctness
non_functional_requirements: Document uses clear English, consistent with docs/conventions.md
All commands are in code blocks for easy copy-paste
definition_of_done: A human can execute the smoke test without additional knowledge
Verification of two different bot identities is documented

decision_log.decision: The smoke test is a manual procedure documented in docs/e2e.md, not an automated test script
decision_log.rationale: Specified in §2.12: 'Ein dediziertes Sandbox-Repo golemic_e2e mit Wegwerf-Issues. Manuell getriggerter Smoke-Test' and backlog: 'Automatisierung des E2E-Laufs' is out of scope

decision_log.decision: The sandbox repo is named golemic_e2e
decision_log.rationale: Specified in §2.12 and backlog: 'Vorbereitung des Sandbox-Repos golemic_e2e'

decision_log.decision: The smoke test document is based on the specification in I1.11 of the backlog
decision_log.rationale: docs/backlog.md section I1.11 specifies the smoke test requirements

decision_log.decision: E2E smoke test is a manual procedure, not part of the automated test suite
decision_log.rationale: §2.12 of the architecture document defines the E2E smoke test as a manual procedure against a dedicated sandbox repo

codebase_evidence.finding: Backlog item specifies: docs/e2e.md, sandbox repo golemic_e2e, preflight + run, expected outcome, teardown
E2E smoke test is defined as a manual procedure against a dedicated sandbox repo, not part of the normal test suite
